jobs.title: County Administrator
jobs.job_summary: This position is responsible for assisting the commissioners with the overall management of the county government, including overseeing the personnel, budgeting, purchasing and grants functions of the government.
major_duties: Assists the commissioners by planning, directing, and supervising the daily operations of the county government.
Acts as liaison between the commissioners and the public by responding to inquiries and resolving conflicts.
Acts as liaison between county departments and various federal, state, and local government agencies.
Develops materials for commission meetings and implements decisions made by the commissioners; oversees the maintenance of minutes and records of meetings.
Insures compliance with all contracts entered into by the county and all ordinances and resolutions adopted by the commission.

Serves as personnel director for the county by performing duties such as advertising openings, administering employee benefits programs, processing complaints or grievances, and overseeing the hiring of all new employees.
Serves as liaison between the commission and department heads; oversees the work of department heads and office personnel.
Manages insurance policies for the county, including auto, liability, property, workers' compensation, and health.
Assists with the preparation of the county budget by consulting with department heads and commissioners, making recommendations, and recommending tax levies.
Investigates and secures available grant funds; monitors the execution of grants.
Serves as county purchasing agent and monitors the county procurement system.
Drafts correspondence, memoranda, speeches, resolutions, ordinances and agreements for the commissioners.
Employs all personnel of the county under the jurisdiction of the Board of Commissioners.
Provides reports and information to the media and general public as requested.
Performs other related duties as assigned.
knowledge_required: Knowledge of the principles and practices of public administration.
Knowledge of the county code of ordinances and other regulations, policies, and procedures.
Knowledge of budget preparation and public financial management principles.
Knowledge of grant application procedures and administrative process.
Knowledge of the functions, organization, and operations of all county departments.
Knowledge of the principles and practices of effective public relations.
Knowledge of state and federal laws in all areas applicable to county government, including unemployment, wage and hour, EEOC, taxes, workers' compensation, pensions, and others.
Skill in developing contacts with other government agencies.
Skill in utilizing appropriate management principles and techniques.
Skill in use of computer software programs.
Skill in verbal and written communication.
jobs.supervisory_controls: The Board of Commissioners assigns work in terms of standing goals, objectives and priorities. The employee must use much judgment in deciding how to accomplish goals. Work is reviewed through conferences, reports and observation of department and government activities.
jobs.guidelines: Guidelines include all county ordinances, applicable state and federal laws and regulations, grant contracts and guidelines, county policies and procedures, and directives from the commission. These guidelines require judgment, selection, and interpretation in application. This position develops departmental guidelines.
jobs.complexity: This position consists of varied administrative and managerial duties.
jobs.scope_and_effect: The purpose of this position is to assist the Board of Commissioners by managing the daily operation of the county government. Successful performance facilitates the work of all county departments, assures that county services are delivered, assures that county records are properly maintained, and enhances the image of the county.
jobs.personal_contacts: Contacts are typically with co-workers, employees in all county departments, elected officials, state employees, representatives of business and civic organizations, and the general public.
jobs.purpose_of_contacts: Contacts are typically to give and exchange information, resolve problems, provide services, motivate and influence personnel, and negotiate and settle matters.
jobs.physical_demands: The work is typically performed with the employee sitting at a desk or table.
jobs.work_environment: The work is typically performed in an office.
jobs.supervisory_and_management_responsibilit: This position has direct supervision over Public Works Director, EMS/EMA Director, Recreation Director, Senior Center Director, County Clerk, and Deputy County Clerk.
minimum_qualifications: Knowledge and level of competency commonly associated with the completion of a master's degree in a course of study related to occupational field.

Experience sufficient to thoroughly understand the diverse objectives and functions of the subunits in the division/department in order to direct and coordinate work within the division/department, usually interpreted to require three to five years of related experience.
Possession of or ability to readily obtain a valid driver's license issued by the State of Georgia for the type of vehicle or equipment operated.

jobs.title: County Clerk
jobs.job_summary: This position is responsible for providing administrative assistance to the County Administrator and Board of Commissioners for the overall administration of county government.
major_duties: Assists the County Administrator with the overall administration of county government.

Serves as personnel and payroll clerk:  maintains employee files and time records, calculates salary changes, prepares bank deposits, processes garnishments, prepares quarterly federal reports, and prepares year-end payroll reports and W-2 forms.

Compiles the agenda for Board of Commissioners meetings, develops supporting meeting materials, attends commission meetings, compiles and records meeting minutes, and responds to requests for information regarding meeting proceedings.
Prepares and submits reimbursement requests to proper agencies.

Serves as financial officer for the county:  maintains all records involving the county general and payroll funds, including preparing regular reports of the financial condition of the county; receives monies, calculates deposits, and posts to various revenue accounts; and prepares trial balance for auditor as requested.
Submits workers compensation claims and invoices to insurance carrier.
Supervises the work of state detainees to ensure building and grounds maintenance and housekeeping duties are performed.
Maintains office supply and janitorial supply inventories.
Answers the telephone and greets the general public.
Provides clerical assistance by typing purchase orders and correspondence as requested.
Performs back-up of computer files and determines computer software and hardware problems.
Performs other related duties as assigned.
knowledge_required: Knowledge of the principles and practices of accounting, especially as applied to local government.
Knowledge of the principles and techniques of modern office management.
Knowledge of county ordinances, policies, and procedures.
Skill in utilizing such modern office equipment as a copier, typewriter, facsimile machine, calculator, and personal computer.
Skill in accounting and bookkeeping.
Skill in maintaining records.
Skill in verbal and written communication.
jobs.supervisory_controls: The County Administrator assigns work in terms of very general instructions. Work is reviewed for the nature and propriety of the final results.
jobs.guidelines: Guidelines include relevant federal and state laws, accepted accounting and auditing principles for government organizations, and county ordinances, policies, and procedures.  These guidelines require judgment, selection and interpretation in application.
jobs.complexity: This position consists of varied duties in assisting the County Administrator in administering the county government.
jobs.scope_and_effect: The purpose of this position is to assist with the overall administration of county government.  Successful performance facilitates the operations of all county departments and helps assure that county operations are aligned with generally accepted accounting practices.
jobs.personal_contacts: Contacts are typically with co-workers, other county employees, elected officials, bankers, attorneys, members of local civic organizations, federal and state officials, contractors, and the general public.
jobs.purpose_of_contacts: Contacts are typically to give and exchange information, motivate personnel, resolve problems, and provide services.
jobs.physical_demands: The work is typically performed with the employee sitting at a desk or table.
jobs.work_environment: The work is typically performed in an office.
jobs.supervisory_and_management_responsibilit: This position has functional supervision over Deputy County Clerk.
minimum_qualifications: Knowledge and level of competency commonly associated with the completion of a baccalaureate degree in a course of study related to occupational field.
Experience sufficient to thoroughly understand the work of subordinate positions to be able to answer questions and resolve problems, usually associated with one to three years experience or service.

jobs.title: Deputy County Clerk

major_duties: Processes accounts payable for the county: matches purchase orders against invoices and prepares payments for bills, and posts.
types agenda; attends monthly Board of Commissioners meetings; compiles and records minutes.
Processes accounts receivable for the county: receives monies, prepares deposits and posts to proper accounts; issues invoices to vendors of solid waste transfer station.

Assists the County Administrator and County Clerk with the administration of the department and county; prepares correspondence, makes telephone calls, gathers documents as needed; distributes and opens daily mail.
Issues invoices for county trash bags sold by local merchants and receives monies from sale of bags.
Maintains computer records of woprk performed by county maintenance shop to all county vehicals and equipment.

Also provides administrative support and special projects for the County Administrator and Board of Commissioners.

Determines and implements standards and decisions regarding changes to networks, systems, and applications; provides technical expertise
Manages vendor relationships to maximize value to the County and negotiates technology-related contracts
Provides enabling technologies to improve County processes, increase revenue, and reduce costs
Manages IT infrastructure, architecture, applications development, networks, operations, and employee support
Provides Internet access to public records for the County and residents
Manages development and delivery of internal IT training
Provides administrative support to the County Administrator and Board of Commissioners
Assists the County Administrator with special projects
Assists with grant applications
Assists with budget preparation and administration
Maintains, develops, and updates the County website
Develops and maintains electronic filing systems for County records
Performs other duties as required

Knowledge of the basic operations of all county departments
Knowledge of County policies and procedures
Skill in planning and organizing; ability to work independently
Skill in composing correspondence
Skill in written and oral communication
Skill in information technology hardware and software
Skill in maintaining websites

jobs.guidelines: Guidelines include County ordinances, relevant state and federal laws, and supervisory instructions. These require judgment, selection, and interpretation in application.
jobs.complexity: The duties of this position are technically complex. Successful performance facilitates County operations and requires independent work on new and varied tasks.
jobs.personal_contacts: Contacts are typically with the Board of Commissioners, County Administrator, department heads, constitutional officers, other county employees, and the general public.
jobs.purpose_of_contacts: Contacts are typically to give and exchange information, present technical processes, resolve problems, and provide services.
jobs.physical_demands: The work is typically performed while sitting at a desk with intermittent standing or stooping.
jobs.work_environment: The work is typically performed in an office.

minimum_qualifications: Sufficient experience to understand the principles relevant to the position, typically associated with completion of a four-year degree or equivalent experience
Possession of or ability to obtain a valid Georgia driver’s license
Proficiency in IT-related hardware and software
desirable_qualifications: Four-year undergraduate degree in a related field

jobs.title: Chief Deputy Clerk
jobs.job_summary: This position is responsible for providing administrative and clerical support to the Office of the Clerk of Superior Court.
major_duties: Performs case management:  maintains computer calendar on criminal and civil cases, prepares notices, subpoenas and other court-related papers.
Files civil and criminal papers.
Prepares monthly and quarterly financial reports for special accounts and trust funds.
Provides assistance to the public by answering the telephone and assisting persons with obtaining copies of records.
Processes and types applications for notary public commissions and passports.

Provides bookkeeping support to the court by preparing bank deposits, preparing invoices for copies, accepting payments for court services and fines, and balancing the daily ledger at the end of each day.
Attends court for jury selection.
Performs the duties of the Clerk of Superior Court in his or her absence.

Knowledge of legal terminology and required record-keeping procedures.
Knowledge of modern office procedures.

Skill in operating such standard office equipment as a computer, typewriter, calculator, copier and facsimile machine.

jobs.title: E-911 Communications Officer
jobs.job_summary: This position is responsible for receiving, processing, disseminating emergency and non-emergency information received from the public and public safety departments associated with E-911 along with maintaining records of the E-911 communications center.
major_duties: Answers E-911 phone system in a professional manor per approved E-911 policies and procedures.
Operates the E-911 CAD (computer aided dispatch) system per approved E-911 policies and procedures.
Receives and electronically documents information received through either emergency or administrative lines per approved E-911 policies and procedures.
Receives and electronically documents information received via radio systems per approved E-911 policies and procedures.
Receives and disseminates information for public notification per approved E-911 policies and procedures.
Communicates emergency and non-emergency information via radio, phone, or other means to all public safety and other necessary agencies per approved E-911 policies and procedures.
Maintains all E-911 and departmental specific logs per approved E-911 policies and procedures.
Stays current with state-of-the-art communication methods, training and equipment.
Assures that new information for database files, Law, EMS , fire, etc. are reported to communication director as needed.
Responds to emergency operations plans for combating various hazards and situations.

Maintains a comprehensive, current knowledge and awareness of applicable laws, regulations, policies procedures; maintains an awareness of new equipment, procedures and advancements in the profession; reads professional literature; maintains professional affiliations participates in continuing education activities; attends staff meetings, workshops and seminars as appropriate.
Performs other related duties as assigned.
knowledge_required: Knowledge of all aspects emergency communications.
Techniques and methods of public notification.
Knowledge of NCIC rules and regulations.
Knowledge of county employment rules, E-911 SOP, rules and regulations.
Skill in supervising and training others.
Skill in implementing emergency procedures for a variety of situations.
Skill in verbal and written communication.
Skill in computer using CAD (computer aided dispatch), word processing and spreadsheet applications.
jobs.supervisory_controls: This person reports to the E-911 Communication Director who assigns work in general and specific terms with oversight provided by the Emergency Services Director under advice from the E-911 Advisory committee, who offers evaluation in the departmental specific performance of their job. The employee typically works independently, relying on training, past experience, and use of judgment to perform their duties.
jobs.guidelines: Guidelines include accepted management and supervisory techniques, protocols and regulations, federal and state laws, and county and E-911 policies and procedures.  Additional guidelines include written and verbal orders from superiors. These guidelines require judgment, selection, and interpretation in application.
jobs.complexity: This position consists of related duties in providing vital information in emergency situations. The variety of duties preformed contributes to the difficulty of the work.
jobs.scope_and_effect: The purpose of this position is to provide state-of-the-art emergency communications for both public safety agencies and citizens of the county. Successful performance affects the operation of the department, the health and well-being of county citizens, and the public image of the department.
jobs.personal_contacts: Contacts are typically with co-workers, fire department officials, law enforcement officials, emergency medical service personnel, and the general public.
jobs.purpose_of_contacts: Contacts are typically to provide services, exchange information, and resolve problems.
jobs.physical_demands: The work is typically performed with the employee sitting, standing, walking, bending, and stooping.  The employee must use equipment requiring a high degree of dexterity, and distinguish between shades of color.
jobs.work_environment: The work is typically performed in an office or dispatch facility.
jobs.work_hours: Weekly work schedules may vary slightly due to scheduling but will be primarily be 40 hours per week.
minimum_qualifications: High school diploma or GED.
Police Officers Standards and Training certified Communication Officer or eligible for certification.
Emergency Medical Dispatch certified Officer or eligible for certification.
TACT certified Officer or eligible for certification.
Possession of or ability to readily obtain a valid driver’s license issued by the State of Georgia for the type of vehicle or equipment operated.
Ability to correctly type minimum of 20 words per minute.
desirable_qualifications: Graduate of a two or four year accredited college course in communications.
Experience in Emergency Medical Services communications.
The ability to correctly type 60 to 80 words per minute.

jobs.title: EMS/EMA Director
jobs.job_summary: This position is responsible for providing emergency health care to the citizens of Hart County, which includes directing the activities of the Emergency Medical Services, conducting administrative activities, and assuring that patients get state-of-the-art emergency care.  This position also coordinates the provision of emergency management.
major_duties: Plans, organizes, directs, and supervises the work force of the Emergency Medical Services.
Conducts personnel administration activities within the Emergency Medical Services, including counseling and disciplining employees, training employees, and evaluating performance.
Develops and implements policies governing county-wide disaster planning,  patient care, standard operating procedures, and employee conduct.
Develops and updates emergency operations plans for such emergencies as fires, blizzards, tornadoes, floods, and hazardous materials situations.

Assures that personnel, vehicles and equipment meet or exceed accepted standards by assuring that training files are maintained and by inspecting and replacing/repairing equipment and vehicles as necessary.
Reviews all paperwork involved in maintaining emergency response records for accuracy and completeness.
Reviews necessary paperwork as required, including monthly DHR reports, invoices for patient billing, and ledger analysis reports.
Develops budget for Emergency Medical Services and Emergency Management Agency, submits purchase requests, and maintains records of expenditures and revenue.
Provides in-service training and instruction to subordinates and evaluates them for licensing in various emergency care skills; adjusts training programs as necessary.
Coordinates training from GEMA for local agencies.
Makes presentations to schools, community organizations, and others.
Acts as liaison between department and other county officials, state and federal agencies, and other medical/emergency personnel.
Participates in professional development activities and assures that subordinate personnel do likewise.
Acts as a paramedic on emergency calls.
Performs other related duties as assigned.
knowledge_required: Knowledge of EMS systems, including emergency dispatch/communication procedures.
Knowledge of basic and advanced emergency medical and life-sustaining techniques, including cardiac care, trauma and shock care, and ventilation and other CPR techniques.
Knowledge of basic algebra, pharmacology, and chemistry for the administration of drug modalities.
Knowledge of medical terminology.
Knowledge of county employment rules, EMT training, and DHR rules and regulations.
Knowledge of insurance, Medicare, and Medicaid laws and related forms.
Knowledge of proper format on many specialized forms.
Skill in personnel administration and in supervising and training others.
Skill in planning, coordinating, and implementing emergency procedures for a variety of situations.
Skill in interpreting and applying rules and regulations of various regulatory agencies.
Skill in basic accounting procedures.
Skill in verbal and written communication.
Skill in public speaking.
jobs.supervisory_controls: The County Administrator assigns work in terms of departmental goals and objectives.  The employee typically works independently and seeks assistance only if a problem arises.  The work is reviewed through conferences, reports, and observation of overall departmental operations.
jobs.guidelines: Guidelines include established medical procedures and protocols, DHR rules and regulations, and federal, state, and county policies and procedures.  These guidelines require judgment, selection, and interpretation in application.  This position develops departmental guidelines.
jobs.complexity: This position consists of a variety of duties in administering the operations of  emergency medical services of the county.   The variety of potential emergencies contributes to the difficulty of the work.
jobs.scope_and_effect: The purpose of this position is to provide state-of-the-art emergency medical service for the county and to coordinate emergency management functions.  The position has a considerable impact on the operation of the department and on the health and well-being of county residents.
jobs.personal_contacts: Contacts are typically with patients, families and friends of patients, EMTs, physicians, other medical personnel, other emergency personnel, county commissioners, law enforcement officials, and the general public.
jobs.purpose_of_contacts: Contacts are typically to provide services, exchange information, resolve problems, motivate and influence others, and negotiate and settle matters.
jobs.physical_demands: The work is typically performed with the employee sitting, standing, walking, bending, and stooping.  The employee must frequently lift light and heavy objects, climb ladders, use tools or equipment requiring a high degree of dexterity, and distinguish between shades of color.
jobs.work_environment: The work is typically performed in an office or stockroom, except while at an emergency scene, where the employee may be exposed to noise, dust, dirt, grease, machinery with moving parts, contagious or infectious diseases, and occasional inclement weather. jobs.title: Maintenance Supervisor
jobs.job_summary: This position is responsible for diagnosing and correcting mechanical problems on a variety of heavy equipment and emergency vehicles.
major_duties: Oversees daily shop operations; supervises employees in absence of Maintenance Superintendent.
Assists with scheduling employees and jobs.

Repairs and maintains emergency vehicles for the county, including repairing engines, fuel systems, electrical systems, brakes, lights, two-way radios, sirens, water pumps, and computer and air-conditioning systems.

Performs preventive maintenance by checking antifreeze, belts, batteries, brakes, lights, hydraulic systems, tires and safety equipment on a variety of diesel-powered heavy equipment, including bulldozers, motor graders, endloaders, asphalt rollers, paving spreaders, gravel spreaders, forklifts, dirt pans, backhoes, tractors, boom trucks, dump trucks, and refuse trucks.
Performs corrective maintenance procedures, including transmission, hydraulic, electrical, and various degrees of engine repair.
Performs vehicle and equipment maintenance procedures such as tune-ups, oil and filter changes, and heating and air-conditioning repairs.
Utilizes welding equipment to fabricate and build water and storage tanks for fire trucks, and repair bushhogs and other equipment as needed.
Performs a variety of engine diagnosis procedures.
Overhauls, troubleshoots, and repairs air-conditioning systems using CFC-R12 refrigerant.
Performs machine work on lathe and drill press to make parts that are unavailable or cost-prohibitive.
Performs body work on county vehicles to repair dents, scratches, and holes; uses bondo and fiberglass to repair trucks; sands, primes, paints and seals vehicles.
Performs electrical wiring and installation for lights, buildings, and machinery, including 110-220-440, single-phase, and 3-phase voltage.
Orders and picks up parts from vendors as needed.
Maintains a clean work area.
Performs other related duties as assigned.
knowledge_required: Knowledge of engine operation and mechanics for a variety of vehicles and heavy equipment.
Knowledge of hydraulic system operation and repair procedures.
Knowledge of repair principles and procedures for gasoline- and diesel-powered engines.
Knowledge of air-conditioning system operation and repair and reclamation procedures.
Knowledge of electrical system repair procedures.
Skill in diagnosing mechanical problems for a wide variety of vehicles and equipment.
Skill in performing preventive and corrective maintenance procedures.
Skill in the use of a variety of hand and power tools used in repairing heavy equipment.
Skill in the operation of a variety of heavy equipment.
jobs.supervisory_controls: The Maintenance Superintendent assigns work in terms of general instructions requiring judgment in determining what needs to be done and how it is to be accomplished.  Work is reviewed for compliance with procedures and the nature and propriety of final results.
jobs.guidelines: Guidelines include federal and state regulations, county policies and procedures, environmental regulations, work orders, and service and repair manuals.  These guidelines are generally clear and specific, but may require interpretation in application.
jobs.complexity: The work consists of supervisory and technical duties in the repair of automotive and heavy equipment.  The need to be knowledgeable about many types of heavy equipment and vehicles, each having its own set of specifications, contributes to the complexity of the work.
jobs.scope_and_effect: The purpose of this position is to supervise daily shop operations and repair and maintain all heavy equipment and vehicles for the county.  Successful performance helps ensure safe, effective, and available equipment and vehicles for the county government.
jobs.personal_contacts: Contacts are typically with co-workers, other county employees, parts vendors, and employees who bring equipment to the shop.
jobs.purpose_of_contacts: Contacts are typically to exchange information, resolve problems, and provide services.
jobs.physical_demands: The work is typically performed with the employee sitting, standing, walking, bending, crouching, or stooping.  The employee must frequently lift light and heavy objects, climb ladders, use tools requiring a high degree of dexterity, and be able to distinguish between shades of color.
jobs.work_environment: The work is typically performed outdoors and in a garage.  The employee is exposed to noise, dust, dirt, grease, machinery with moving parts, irritating chemicals, paint fumes, and occasional inclement weather.  The use of protective devices is required.
jobs.supervisory_and_management_responsibilit: This position has functional supervision over Mechanic, Mechanic/Welder, Service Truck Operator, and Shop Inventory Clerk.
minimum_qualifications: Knowledge and level of competency commonly associated with completion of specialized training in the field of work, in addition to basic skills typically associated with a high school education.
Experience sufficient to thoroughly understand the work of subordinate positions to be able to answer questions and resolve problems, usually associated with one to three years experience or service.
Possession of or ability to readily obtain a valid driver=s license issued by the State of Georgia for the type of vehicle or equipment operated.

jobs.title: Chief Deputy Clerk
jobs.job_summary: This position is responsible for providing clerical and record-keeping support to the Probate Court.
major_duties: Assists the Probate Judge:  types correspondence, prepares court orders, maintains appointment calendar, schedules training, and performs other tasks as requested.
Receives documents for filing in estate court, publishes citations as required, and calls cases before Judge on court day.
Records, dockets, and prepares certified copies of the probate of wills, administrations, and guardianships; receives wills of living persons for safekeeping.
Receives pleas and monies for state and county traffic citations.
Accepts and receipts all fines, fees, and service payments; prepares bank deposits.
Issues, records, and files firearms licenses.
Issues certified copies of marriage certificates, birth certificates, and death certificates.
Issues disinterment, reinterment, and transit permits.
Issues, types, records, and files marriage licenses.
Prepares and issues affidavits for involuntary hospitalization.

Assists with the coordination of the elections process by preparing ballots, preparing polling places, consolidating the paperwork required by the state following elections, maintaining election supplies, and preparing campaign financial disclosure reports; maintains and records oaths of elected officials.
Issues firework permits.
Issues senior citizen honorary hunting and fishing licenses.
Maintains and orders office supplies.
Performs other related duties as assigned.
knowledge_required: Knowledge of county policies and procedures.
Knowledge of the laws and procedures governing the functions of the Probate Court.
Knowledge of modern office procedures.
Knowledge of federal election laws and state traffic laws.
Skill in typing, filing, and indexing court records.
Skill in operating such office equipment as a computer, typewriter, calculator, copier, facsimile machine, and paper shredder.
Skill in basic mathematics and bookkeeping.
Skill in dealing with the public.
Skill in verbal and written communication.
jobs.supervisory_controls: The Probate Judge assigns work in terms of general instructions.  Work is reviewed for accuracy and the nature and propriety of the final results.
jobs.guidelines: Guidelines include the Official Code of Georgia Annotated, Department of Human Resources regulations, state Department of Public Safety regulations, Probate Judges Handbook, election procedures, and departmental policies and procedures.  These guidelines are clear and specific, but require some interpretation in application.
jobs.complexity: This position consists of related clerical and administrative tasks.  The variety of court processes contributes to the complexity of the work.
jobs.scope_and_effect: The purpose of this position is to assist the Probate Judge in providing court services to the public.  Successful performance helps ensure the efficient collection of traffic citation revenues and the accuracy and completeness of vital records for the county.
jobs.personal_contacts: Contacts are typically with co-workers, law enforcement personnel, state employees, members of the legal profession, the general public, and other county employees.
jobs.purpose_of_contacts: Contacts are typically to give or exchange information and provide services.
jobs.physical_demands: The work is typically performed with the employee sitting, standing or stooping.
jobs.work_environment: The work is typically performed in an office.
jobs.supervisory_and_management_responsibilit: This position has functional supervision over Probate Clerk.
minimum_qualifications: Knowledge and level of competency commonly associated with completion of specialized training in the field of work, in addition to basic skills typically associated with a high school education.
Experience sufficient to thoroughly understand the work of subordinate positions to be able to answer questions and resolve problems, usually associated with one to three years experience or service.

major_duties: Receives, dockets, and processes state and county traffic citations; accepts and receipts monies for fines, enters data to the computer, records case dispositions, and disburses funds received to the appropriate authorities.

Answers the telephone and assists the general public.

Assists with the coordination of the elections process by ordering supplies, notifying precinct workers, consolidating the paperwork required by the state following elections, and preparing worker time cards.

jobs.physical_demands: The work is typically performed with the employee sitting, standing, walking, bending, crouching or stooping. The employee must occasionally lift light objects.

minimum_qualifications: Ability to read, write and perform mathematical calculations at a level commonly associated with the completion of high school or equivalent.

jobs.title: Public Works Director
jobs.job_summary: This position is responsible for planning and directing the operation of the department, including the work of the Road, Maintenance Shop, and Solid Waste departments.
major_duties: Plans and implements the goals, objectives, and strategies for all Public Works departments; supervises the work of personnel involved in road and bridge system construction and maintenance tasks.
Supervises the work of personnel involved in solid waste and recycling collection and disposal.
Interviews, hires, trains, evaluates, rewards and disciplines subordinates; maintains personnel files on all departmental employees.
Conducts regular staff meetings to review progress, accomplishments, budgets and plans for Public Works departments.
Prepares proposed annual operating budgets and monitors expenditures under current operating budgets for all Public Works departments.
Responds to inquiries and complaints from the general public.
Reviews and approves equipment requests and specifications for equipment purchases, solicits bids, and submits purchase requests to the County Administrator for approval.
Submits funding requests regarding county road construction and maintenance policy to the DOT.
Maintains project status files to include all current and planned Road and Solid Waste projects; submits monthly status reports to the County Administrator on work completed.
Serves as fleet manager for all county departments, including supervising the equipment maintenance program and the preparation of equipment specifications.
Administers and supervises the Land Use Development Standards ordinance, Erosion Control program, and National Flood Insurance program.
Provides technical engineering assistance to the Water and Sewer, and Industrial Development Authorities.
Performs other related duties as assigned.
knowledge_required: Knowledge of techniques, equipment and supplies used in road and bridge construction, maintenance, and repair.
Knowledge of proper solid waste management practices.
Knowledge of county operating policies, procedures, ordinances and codes, including budgeting and purchasing procedures.
Knowledge of proper equipment operation, maintenance and design specifications.
Skill in reading blue prints and schematics.
Skill in scheduling, assigning and prioritizing work.
Skill in dealing with the public.
Skill in developing and reviewing future plans for development.
Skill in the use of computer drafting and spreadsheet programs.
Skill in interpreting and applying engineering and construction-related standards.
jobs.supervisory_controls: The County Administrator assigns work in terms of departmental goals and objectives.  Work is reviewed through reports, conferences, and observation of departmental activities.
jobs.guidelines: Guidelines include county operating policies and procedures, EPA regulations, DOT manual, and standard operating procedures.  These guidelines require judgment, selection and interpretation in application.  This position develops departmental guidelines.
jobs.complexity: The work consists of varied planning, administrative, and supervisory duties.
jobs.scope_and_effect: The purpose of this position is to direct the work of personnel involved in road and bridge construction and maintenance and solid waste collection.  Successful performance in this position helps ensure the repair and maintenance of county roads, the proper collection and disposal of solid waste, and the efficient operation of the department.
jobs.personal_contacts: Contacts are typically with co-workers, utility company personnel, state DOT personnel, government agency representatives, vendors, other county employees, and the general public.
jobs.purpose_of_contacts: Contacts are typically to exchange information, resolve problems, motivate personnel, and provide services.
jobs.physical_demands: The work is typically performed with the employee sitting at a desk or table.
jobs.work_environment: The work is typically performed in an office and  outdoors.  On occasion the employee is exposed to noise, dust, dirt, grease, inclement weather, and machinery with moving parts.  The use of protective devices such as masks, goggles or gloves may be required.
jobs.supervisory_and_management_responsibilit: This position has direct supervision over Road Superintendent, Maintenance Shop Superintendent, and Solid Waste Superintendent Environmental Compliance Officer, 911 Coordinator, and Public Works Specialist.
minimum_qualifications: Knowledge and level of competency commonly associated with the completion of a baccalaureate degree in a course of study related to occupational field.

Sufficient experience to understand the basic principles relevant to the major duties of the position, usually associated with the completion of an apprenticeship/ internship or having had a similar position for one to two years.
Possession of or ability to readily obtain a valid driver=s license issued by the State of Georgia for the type of vehicle or equipment operated.
desirable_qualifications: BS in Civil Engineering from an accredited university
Professional Engineer Registration in State of Georgia.

jobs.title: Director
jobs.job_summary: This position is responsible for the overall administration of the programs, operations, and facilities of the department.
major_duties: Plans, develops, and directs the operation of recreation programs, activities, and facilities; supervises the work of office, athletic, and maintenance staff.
Plans and supervises the construction, maintenance, repair, and purchase of departmental facilities and equipment.
Secures bids for sports equipment, uniforms, photographs, and other goods and services needed by the department.
Prepares and administers the annual department budget; monitors expenditures under the current budget.
Schedules, coordinates, and supervises youth and adult sports activities and programs.
Coordinates departmental programs with other county agencies and schools.
Provides information to the public about department programs; makes presentations to groups and speaks with reporters and supervises the preparation of news releases and bulletins.
Serves as a member of local and state recreation boards.
Performs other related duties as assigned.
knowledge_required: Knowledge of the principles and practices of public recreation administration.
Knowledge of theories and techniques of delivery of recreation services to a variety of populations.
Knowledge of the rules and regulations of various sports associations.
Knowledge of recreation facility maintenance.
Knowledge of all relevant laws, county codes, policies and procedures, and departmental safety guidelines.
Knowledge of bookkeeping and budgetary processes and procedures.
Skill in management and supervision.
Skill in planning and organizing.
Skill in public relations and public speaking.
Skill in operating such grounds maintenance equipment as a tractor, riding mower, push mower, and weed eater.
Skill in operating such office equipment as a computer and typewriter.
Skill in verbal and written communication.
jobs.supervisory_controls: The County Administrator assigns work in terms of departmental goals and objectives.  The employee works independently to organize staff and programs so that goals are met.  Work is reviewed through reports, conferences and observation of departmental activities.
jobs.guidelines: Guidelines include relevant federal and state laws, county policies and procedures, Georgia Recreation and Parks Association guidelines, American Sports Association guidelines, Little League rule books, and departmental and safety guidelines.  These guidelines require judgment, selection and interpretation in application.  This position develops departmental guidelines.
jobs.complexity: This position consists of a variety of administrative, public relations, and planning duties.
jobs.scope_and_effect: The purpose of this position is to manage the provision of recreation services to community residents.  Successful performance helps ensure the effective delivery of recreation services, thereby improving the quality of life for citizens and enhancing the image of the county.
jobs.personal_contacts: Contacts are typically with co-workers, elected officials, representatives of professional and community organizations, volunteers, the media, and the general public.
jobs.purpose_of_contacts: Contacts are typically to exchange information, provide services and information, resolve problems, and motivate others.
jobs.physical_demands: The work is typically performed with the employee sitting, standing, or stooping.  The employee occasionally lifts light and heavy objects.
jobs.work_environment: The work is typically performed in an office and at outdoor facilities, where the employee may be exposed to machinery with moving parts, irritating chemicals, and inclement weather.  The use of protective devices may be required.
jobs.supervisory_and_management_responsibilit: This position has direct supervision over Assistant Recreation Director and Secretary.
minimum_qualifications: Knowledge and level of competency commonly associated with the completion of a baccalaureate degree in a course of study related to occupational field.

Experience sufficient to thoroughly understand the diverse objectives and functions of the subunits in the division/department in order to direct and coordinate work within the division/department, usually interpreted to require three to five years of related experience.
Possession of or ability to readily obtain a valid driver=s license issued by the State of Georgia for the type of vehicle or equipment operated.
